Title: Click previews, right click manages
Summary: One rule everywhere: click opens a preview. Right click opens the editor menu.
Body:
## The rule

Click on any image anywhere in the app: opens the file in a new browser tab as a preview.

Right click on any image: opens a context menu. Items depend on which surface you are on:

- **Default** (cards on entity edit pages, dialog graph nodes, list cards): only "Preview".
- **canEdit=true** (image fields on edit pages): "Edit" and "Preview".
- **canEdit=true and canManage=true** (the asset gallery itself, the AssetPicker browse modal): "Edit", "Duplicate", "Delete", "Preview".

## Why split the menu like that

Editing the file is not the user's primary intent on most surfaces. On an NPC card, clicking the portrait should not open a heavy editor modal by accident; it should preview the image, and editing should require the explicit right click + menu pick.

Destructive ops (Duplicate, Delete) are reserved for the dedicated asset surfaces, where the user is in "managing my project's files" mode rather than "viewing this entity". Deleting a file from disk through a portrait thumbnail on an NPC card would be too easy a mistake.

## The launch path

The image editor is launched through a singleton host (`<ImageEditorHost />` mounted at App root), called via the imperative `showImageEditor(mode, options)` API. Any component anywhere can launch it without per page state threading.

When a save lands, the host emits a `Bleepforge:image-saved` window event. The gallery listens and refreshes ahead of the watcher's debounced asset event, for a snappier feel.
